description: Fix the CLI reference and MCP server reference pages to document the --stdio option on opentabs start, the opentabs telemetry command group, and the 'Auto-start via stdio' fourth connection mode. Also update the stale browserToolCount sample value in the /health response example.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add --stdio option to opentabs start in CLI reference
userStories.description: The CLI reference docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx lists only three options for opentabs start (--port, --background, --show-config) but the real CLI has a fourth option --stdio. Add it to the options table with an accurate description.
acceptanceCriteria: The opentabs start options table includes a --stdio row
The --stdio description explains it uses stdio transport so an MCP client can auto-start the server without a separate manual server start
No existing rows in the table are removed or reworded unless they contain factual errors
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx (add --stdio row to the opentabs start options table)

Context:
The actual CLI code at platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts:576-578 registers:
  .option('--stdio', 'Use stdio transport (bridge to existing HTTP server for MCP client auto-start)')

The help text in start.ts says:
  $ opentabs start --stdio

The current docs table at ~line 50-73 of cli.mdx has these rows for opentabs start:
  --port <number>, --background, --show-config

The --stdio option is NOT in the table.

Changes:
Insert a new Table.Row for --stdio after the --show-config row:
  <Table.Row>
    <Table.Cell className="font-medium">`--stdio`</Table.Cell>
    <Table.Cell>—</Table.Cell>
    <Table.Cell>Use stdio transport — the MCP client spawns `opentabs start --stdio` as a subprocess rather than connecting to a running server. The bridge connects to the existing HTTP server on the configured port. Useful for MCP clients that manage server lifecycle automatically.</Table.Cell>
  </Table.Row>

Also add an example in the code block below the table:
  # Auto-start via stdio (for MCP clients that manage server lifecycle)
  opentabs start --stdio

Constraints:
- Do NOT remove or reorder any existing option rows
- Do NOT add any new sections — only insert into the existing options table and examples block

userStories.title: Add opentabs telemetry command to CLI reference
userStories.description: The CLI reference page (cli.mdx) documents all opentabs commands except opentabs telemetry. The telemetry command has three subcommands (status, enable, disable) and is registered in the CLI. Add a documentation section for it after opentabs update.
acceptanceCriteria: The CLI reference page has an 'opentabs telemetry' section
The section documents the status, enable, and disable subcommands
The docs summary line near the top of cli.mdx (which lists plugin subcommands) is updated to reflect that the telemetry command exists if it currently omits it
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/reference/cli.mdx (add opentabs telemetry section)

Context:
platform/cli/src/commands/telemetry.ts registers:
  program.command('telemetry') with .action(() => handleTelemetryStatus())
  telemetry.command('status') - Show telemetry status (anonymous ID, enabled/disabled)
  telemetry.command('enable') - Enable anonymous telemetry by removing config.telemetry=false
  telemetry.command('disable') - Disable by setting config.telemetry=false in config.json

The telemetry docs page (docs/content/docs/reference/telemetry.mdx) already references these commands and links to the telemetry docs page. The CLI reference needs a section so users can find the command from the comprehensive CLI reference.

Look at the end of cli.mdx (after the 'opentabs update' section, before '## Plugin Developer CLI') for where to add the new section.

Changes:
Add a new section between 'opentabs update' and 'Plugin Developer CLI':

## opentabs telemetry

Manage anonymous usage telemetry. Running `opentabs telemetry` with no subcommand shows current status.

```bash
opentabs telemetry [subcommand]
```

Has three subcommands: `status`, `enable`, `disable`.

### opentabs telemetry status

Show whether telemetry is currently enabled and display the anonymous installation ID.

```bash
opentabs telemetry status
```

### opentabs telemetry enable

Enable anonymous telemetry by removing the `telemetry: false` flag from `config.json`.

```bash
opentabs telemetry enable
```

### opentabs telemetry disable

Disable anonymous telemetry by setting `telemetry: false` in `config.json`.

```bash
opentabs telemetry disable
```

See [Telemetry](/docs/reference/telemetry) for details on what is collected and how to opt out via environment variables.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any existing section content
- lastUpdated frontmatter date must be updated to 2026-04-15

userStories.title: Add Auto-start via stdio as fourth connection mode in MCP server reference
userStories.description: The MCP server reference (mcp-server.mdx) documents three connection modes (Full MCP, Gateway MCP, CLI mode) but there is a fourth: Auto-start via stdio. Add it to the connection modes table and add a subsection. Also update the stale browserToolCount: 40 in the /health response sample to a note that the actual count varies.
acceptanceCriteria: The connection modes table in mcp-server.mdx has a fourth row for 'Auto-start via stdio'
A 'Auto-start via stdio' subsection exists with a Claude Code config example using command/args rather than url
The /health response sample no longer shows the stale hardcoded browserToolCount: 40 value (either update to a realistic current value or add a comment that the count varies)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- docs/content/docs/reference/mcp-server.mdx (add 4th connection mode, fix stale sample)

Context:
The auto-start via stdio mode is shown in platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts:271-295 (the printMcpClientConfigs function's Section 3):
  { mcpServers: { opentabs: { command: 'opentabs', args: ['start', '--stdio'] } } }

This mode lets MCP clients (like Claude Code) auto-spawn the server rather than requiring users to manually start it. When using --stdio, opentabs start runs a bridge that connects to the HTTP server on the configured port and speaks MCP over stdio. The client starts the bridge; the bridge connects to the server.

For the connection modes table around lines 13-43, add a fourth row:
  Mode: Auto-start via stdio
  Context cost: Same as Full MCP
  How it works: MCP client spawns `opentabs start --stdio` as a subprocess; bridge relays to running HTTP server.
  Best for: Users who prefer not to manage a background server process.

For the subsection, add after '### CLI Mode' and before '---':

### Auto-start via stdio

No manual `opentabs start` required. Configure your MCP client to spawn `opentabs start --stdio` directly — the client manages the process lifecycle.

For Claude Code, add to `~/.claude.json`:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "opentabs": {
      "command": "opentabs",
      "args": ["start", "--stdio"]
    }
  }
}
```

The bridge connects to the HTTP server already running on the configured port (default 9515). The HTTP server must be running separately — `opentabs start --stdio` is a bridge, not a server.

For the stale browserToolCount: around line 284 in the health response sample:
  "browserToolCount": 40,
Change to:
  "browserToolCount": 76,
Also update "toolCount": 59 to something consistent — since the sample has 2 plugins with toolCounts of 22+19=41 plugin tools plus 76 browser = 117 total, update to:
  "toolCount": 117,
  "browserToolCount": 76,
  "pluginToolCount": 41,

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any existing connection mode descriptions
- lastUpdated frontmatter date must be updated to 2026-04-15
